agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to explore funding for youth scholarships
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a presentation from Richmond Promise, a local non-profit that helps Richmond students access and complete post-secondary education (including trade schools and colleges, not just four-year universities). After the presentation, Council will consider directing staff to explore funding options to support scholarships for Richmond youth. This is a proposed step only — no funding has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now — watch for the Council decision on whether to proceed with funding exploration.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Presentation from Richmond Promise: Helping to build a stronger Richmond by deeply supporting Richmond students to access and successfully complete post-secondary education (not only 4 year college)
•  RECEIVE a presentation from Richmond Promise on the organization’s current scope of services, how it supports youth before, during, and after post-secondary education, and the impact it has achieved to date and to highlight the program’s current needs as it continues to serve Richmond students; and DIRECT staff to explore funding to support scholarships for Richmond youth - Vice Mayor Doria Robinson (510-620-6568).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on opposing CO2 pipelines
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution opposing the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would build a large network of CO2 pipelines through Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties to transport carbon waste for underground storage. Council will hear public comments and debate the resolution at the upcoming meeting. If approved, the resolution would formally state the city's opposition to the pipeline project, citing health and safety risks to residents.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the council meeting or submit written comments to voice your concerns about the proposed CO2 pipelines.

key_dates.label: Council deliberation and public comment on CO2 pipeline resolution

agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting. THE PRESENTATION FOR THIS ITEM WAS RECEIVED ON MAY 5, 2026. THIS ITEM IS FOR PUBLIC COMMENT AND COUNCIL DELIBERATION ONLY.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to hold public hearing on city job vacancies
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to hold a public hearing and receive a presentation on the status of city job vacancies and the city's recruitment and retention efforts in 2025. This is a proposed annual hearing — it has not yet been approved or enacted. The hearing will give the public a chance to learn about staffing gaps and how the city plans to attract and keep workers.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to learn about city staffing challenges and share your input.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.4
Annual Public Hearing on Vacancies
•  HOLD a public hearing and RECEIVE a presentation on the status of vacancies and the City’s recruitment and retention efforts in 2025 – Human Resources (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6803).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on new Master Fee Schedule
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to hold a public hearing and introduce a new ordinance (a proposed law) that would establish new fees for the city's Master Fee Schedule. This means the city is considering changing the fees it charges for various permits, licences, and services. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on it after the public hearing. Business owners who pay city fees should pay attention to this proposal.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit your comments to council before the vote to have your say on the proposed fee changes.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on proposed Master Fee Schedule

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.3
Master Fee Schedule Update
•  HOLD a public hearing; and INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) establishing certain fees for the proposed Master Fee Schedule – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to levy special assessments for unpaid code fines
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to add unpaid administrative citation fees and nuisance abatement costs to property tax bills as special assessments. This means if a property owner has not paid fines for things like bylaw violations or cleaning up a nuisance property, the city could put those charges on the owner's property tax bill. A public hearing will be held before any decision is made. This is a proposed action — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your concerns or support before council votes.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on special assessments for unpaid code fines — Richmond

agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.2
Levy of Special Assessments for Unpaid Administrative Citation and Nuisance Abatement Costs
•  HOLD a public hearing; and ADOPT a resolution authorizing the levy of special assessments against certain properties with unpaid invoices related to administrative citation fees, and nuisance abatement costs pursuant to Richmond Municipal Code Chapters 2.62, 6.38, 9.22, and 11.76 – Community Development Department/Code Enforcement Division (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1279).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond zoning and land-use changes to match state housing law
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would change several sections of the city's zoning code and the General Plan 2030. These changes are meant to bring local rules in line with recent updates to California state housing law, as part of the city's 6th Cycle Housing Element. If approved, the amendments could affect what types of housing can be built where, and how land can be used. A public hearing will be held before the first vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your support or concerns before Council votes.

key_dates.label: Richmond housing element zoning amendments — public hearing and first reading

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.1
General Plan and Zoning Text Amendments
Related to 6th Cycle Housing Element Program
Implementation
•  HOLD a public hearing; INTRODUCE (first reading) an ordinance, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending Articles 15.04.104, 15.04.201, 15.04.202, 15.04.203, 15.04.602, 15.04.607; Sections 15.04.610.180, 15.04.610.360, 15.04.805.040 and 15.04.806.040 of the Richmond Municipal Code (RMC); and ADOPT a resolution, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending the General Plan 2030 Land Use and Urban Design Element, to implement certain programs identified in the City’s adopted 6th Cycle Housing Element to align with recent changes to State Law - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to review draft budget for Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to consider the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and the FY 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan. As part of this ongoing review, council will receive presentations and updates from the Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments. This is a proposed budget — it has not yet been approved. The council will discuss these departmental presentations and any updated financial or operational considerations before making a final decision. Business owners should watch for the outcome, as the approved budget could affect city services, fees, and economic development programs.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor council meetings for the final budget decision — it may affect city fees, permits, or services your business relies on.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.1
Fiscal Year 2026-27 Draft Budget Department Presentations and Budget Updates
•  RECEIVE an update on departmental presentations from Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments as part of the continued review of the draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 FiveYear Capital Improvement Plan, with discussions upon completion of all departmental presentations; and RECEIVE updated financial and operational considerations – City Manager’s Office/Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond sidewalk vendor rules to be updated
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed update to the Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance (Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code). The changes would update administrative procedures, waiver rules, and enforcement mechanisms for sidewalk vendors. This item was first introduced at the April 28, 2026 meeting and continued to the May 5, 2026 meeting. It is now up for a first reading vote. If approved, it would change how vendors get permits, how waivers are handled, and how rules are enforced on Richmond sidewalks.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your sidewalk vending licence or permit.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.f
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $390,000 e-bike contract extension
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract amendment with Charleston Mobility to keep the city's electric bikeshare program running until the end of the current fiscal year. The amendment would add $390,000, bringing the total contract to $3,540,341. This is a temporary extension to give the city time to decide the program's long-term future during the 2026-27 budget process. The money would come from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. This item was previously discussed at council meetings on April 28 and May 5, 2026, and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect access to the e-bikeshare program in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Council vote on e-bikeshare contract amendment

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.e
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).​ This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $1.28M Santa Fe slope fix
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to fund the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project. The plan would take $72,623 from a different slope project (Santa Rita Road), move $205,502 from a bike/pedestrian safety project (Cutting/Hoffman Quick-Build), and pull $1,000,000 from the city's insurance reserve. The total of $1,278,126 would pay for design work, geotechnical studies, and construction to fix a slope on Santa Fe Avenue. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you right now — watch for the council vote on this funding proposal.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.d
Appropriate Funds for the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to RATIFY the transfer of $72,623.62 from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project; APPROPRIATE $205,502.81 from the Quick-Build project funded through California’s Active Transportation Program for Cutting/Hoffman Project to fund design and geotechnical services; and APPROPRIATE $1,000,000 from the Insurance Reserve Fund to the General Capital Fund in the Fiscal Year 2026–27 budget for construction of the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/ Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $3.25M road maintenance project list
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a resolution that would approve the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) project list and appropriate $3,251,983 in RMRA funds to the Public Works Department. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved. If passed, the money would be used for road maintenance and rehabilitation projects in Richmond.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.c
Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Fund Project List
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill (SB) 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) project list and APPROPRIATE $3,251,983 in RMRA funds to SB 1 Fund 1019 - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/Robert Armijo 510-620-5477).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on Complete Streets funding plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed funding allocation plan for Complete Streets Improvements. This plan covers pavement management, streetscape upgrades, and other street improvements. If approved, the plan would direct how money is spent on making streets safer and more accessible for everyone — including pedestrians, cyclists, and drivers. The decision has not been made yet; it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.b
Complete Streets Improvement Elements Project Funding Allocation Plan
•  APPROVE the Complete Streets Improvements (Pavement Management, Streetscape Improvements, etc.) funding allocation plan – Public Works Department (Josef Munoz 510-620-6671/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to accept Lincoln Elementary School safe routes project as complete
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to formally accept the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as finished. If approved, this would authorize the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion (a legal document that marks the project as officially done) and authorize the city engineer to release any remaining bonds and retention (money held back to ensure the work was finished properly). This is a routine step to close out the project and release funds.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.a
Acceptance of the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete; AUTHORIZE the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion; and AUTHORIZE the city engineer to execute and release applicable bonds and retention – Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 621-1612/Daniel Chavarria 620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $7.2M animal control contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services for ongoing animal control services. The contract would be worth up to $7.2 million and cover the 2026–2027 fiscal year, with an option to extend for three more years (2027–2030). This item was carried over from a previous meeting and is up for approval at the next council meeting. If approved, the total contract term would run from July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030. The Police Department is recommending approval.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — watch for council's decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.c
Agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for Ongoing Animal Control Services
•  APPROVE and AUTHORIZE a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department in an amount not to exceed $7,200,000 for the continuation of Animal Control Services for Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an additional three-year term for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030, for a total contract term of July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802). This item was continued from the May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Three-year sole-source contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for police shooting range rentals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year, sole-source contract with the Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals. A sole-source contract means the city would hire this one club without seeking bids from other businesses. The contract would cost up to $25,000 per year, for a total of up to $75,000 over three years, starting July 1, 2026. The contract also includes two optional one-year extensions at $25,000 per year. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners — this is an internal police department contract.

key_dates.label: Proposed contract start date — Richmond Rod and Gun Club rental

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.b
Contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for Shooting Range Facility Rental
•  APPROVE a three-year sole-source contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals in an amount not to exceed $25,000 annually, for a total not to exceed $75,000, for the term July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, with two one-year extension options at $25,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $150K Verizon GPS contract for police vehicles
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Verizon Connect to provide GPS tracking and management for Richmond police vehicles. The contract would run for three years starting July 1, 2026, at a cost not to exceed $150,000, with options to extend for two additional one-year periods at up to $50,000 per year. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved.

key_dates.label: Proposed Verizon GPS contract start date — Richmond Police
Proposed contract end date — Richmond Police GPS

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.a
Contract with Verizon Connect for Police Vehicle GPS Service
•  APPROVE a contract with Verizon Connect for monthly GPS tracking and management in an amount not to exceed $150,000 over a three-year term from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, plus two one-year extension options not to exceed $50,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to add four new seats to Macdonald Avenue task force
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposal to expand the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force by adding four new stakeholder positions. If approved, these seats would be filled through the city's standard boards and commissions process, which is the usual way the city appoints people to advisory groups. This task force helps guide planning and development decisions along the Macdonald Avenue corridor, so the change could affect local businesses and property owners in that area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, consider applying for one of the four new stakeholder positions through the city's boards and commissions process.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.11.e
Expansion of the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force Membership
•  APPROVE the addition of four (4) new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force which will be filled through the standard boards and commissions process—Mayor’s Office (Eduardo Martinez (510-620-6502).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council rules overhaul to improve meeting efficiency
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request to create a special committee (called an ad hoc committee) that would rewrite the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order. The goal is to make council meetings run more smoothly and efficiently. If approved, the mayor would appoint members to this committee, and they would have 60 days to propose specific changes for the full council to vote on later. This is not a done deal — it is a proposal that council will discuss and vote on at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.10.a
Ad-Hoc Committee to Amend City Council Rules of Procedure and Order
•  REQUEST that Mayor Eduardo Martinez appoint a City Council ad hoc committee to work with the city clerk, city attorney, and city manager to develop proposed amendments to the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order and other operational refinements to improve efficiency, for consideration by the full City Council within 60 days of the committee’s appointment - City Clerk’s Office/City Attorney’s Office/City Manager’s Office (Pamela Christian 510-620-6513/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509/Shasa Curl 510-620-6512).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 5% salary adjustment for Richmond city clerk in 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a five percent (5%) salary increase for the city clerk, effective March 2, 2026. This proposed adjustment is part of the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding — a formal agreement that sets pay and working conditions for certain senior city staff. The decision has not been made yet; it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

key_dates.label: Proposed city clerk salary increase takes effect

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.9.a
Annual Compensation Adjustment for the City Clerk
•  APPROVE a five percent (5%) salary adjustment for the city clerk effective March 2, 2026, in accordance with the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding Section 6.4D – Human Resources Department (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6602).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on wildfire preparedness budget
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on recommendations from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee. The committee has presented a written report with ideas to improve the city's wildfire readiness. Council will give direction to staff on which recommendations to fund during the upcoming budget process. The committee will then be disbanded. This is a proposed decision — nothing has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.c
Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee Recommendations
•  RECEIVE a written presentation from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee; PROVIDE DIRECTION to staff on the City Council’s preferred recommendation to improve the City’s wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts during the budget checklist on proposed appropriations; and ACKNOWLEDGE the disbanding of the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee – Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on EMS funding amendment with Contra Costa Health Services
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to an interagency agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for the Emergency Medical Services (EMS) program. If approved, the amendment would extend the current agreement through June 30, 2027, and accept an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved or enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — this is a proposed council decision that does not directly affect business operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.b
Interagency Agreement Amendment with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program
•  ADOPT a resolution to: (1) APPROVE and AUTHORIZE the city manager, or designee to execute an amendment to the agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program, extending the term of the current interagency agreement through June 30, 2027; and (2) ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE funds in an amount not to exceed an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding from the Contra Costa County Health Services, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Deputy Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on disaster aid application officials
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to designate specific officials who would be authorized to apply for state and federal disaster financial assistance. If approved, the Fire Department would be able to submit applications to the California Governor's Office of Emergency Services for funds under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.a
Designating Officials Authorized to Execute and Submit Application for Federal and State Financial Disaster Assistance
•  ADOPT a resolution designating officials that are authorized to execute and submit applications to the State of California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services for state financial assistance under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act – Fire Department (Shasa Curl 510-620-6512/Emily Combs 510-620-6740/Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: City of Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy up for approval
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider adopting a new Donation Acceptance Policy at an upcoming meeting. This proposed policy would set rules for how the City of Richmond can accept donations from individuals, businesses, or organizations. If approved, it would create a formal process for reviewing and approving donations to ensure they align with city values and legal requirements. The policy is not yet in effect — it is up for a vote by Council.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action needed. Watch for the Council decision — if approved, the policy will affect how donations are handled.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.c
Donation Acceptance Policy
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the City of Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Jerry Chak 510-620-6569).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on 0.14% pension tax rate for 2026-27
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would set the tax rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund at 0.14 percent for the 2026-27 fiscal year. This is a first reading of the ordinance, meaning it has been introduced but not yet approved. If passed, this tax rate would apply to property taxes to help fund the city's pension obligations. The decision is scheduled for an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this proposed tax rate could affect your property taxes if approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.b
Setting Tax Rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-27
•  INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) setting the tax rate for the Tax Override Pension Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-2027 at 0.14 percent – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/ Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to write off $535,499 in uncollected debts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to write off $535,499.12 in unpaid accounts receivable. 'Write-off' means the city would officially give up trying to collect these debts and remove them from its books. This does not cancel the debt — the city could still try to collect in the future — but it is a signal that the city considers these amounts unlikely to be paid. The proposal covers delinquent (overdue) and uncollectible accounts from various city services. A vote is expected at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — this is an internal city accounting decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.a
Approval of Write-Off of Uncollectible Accounts Receivables
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the write-off of delinquent and/or uncollectible accounts receivable in the amount of $535,499.12 – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $60K increase for facade grant program
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract amendment with the Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center. If approved, the amendment would increase the total funding for the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program by $60,000, bringing the total to $400,000. The program helps small businesses pay for exterior building improvements like new signs, paint, or storefront repairs. The contract would also be extended to June 30, 2027. This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet and will be decided at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, eligible Richmond small businesses can apply for facade improvement funding.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.b
Contract Amendment with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center for Small Business Beautification Pilot Program
•  APPROVE the first amendment to the contract with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center to increase the total contract amount by $60,000 for a revised amount not to exceed $400,000, extend the term through June 30, 2027, and support payment administration for eligible businesses participating in the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program - Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Downtown Richmond business district levy for FY 2026-2027
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to levy assessments on property owners in the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District (PBID). If approved, the assessment would be collected through the County of Contra Costa property tax rolls for the 2026-2027 fiscal year. This means property owners in the downtown area would pay an extra charge on their property tax bill to fund improvements and services in the district.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this levy will appear on your property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.a
Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District Fiscal Year 2026–2027 Assessment Levy
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the City of Richmond to levy assessments on property owners subject to the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District through the County of Contra Costa property tax rolls for Fiscal Year 2026–2027 – Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to accept $35,000 Chevron grant for street outreach
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider accepting a $35,000 grant from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation. If approved, the money would go to the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to help pay for street outreach and mobile crisis response teams. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.a
Grant Award from Chevron Community Engagement Foundation
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE a $35,000 grant award from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation for the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to Enhance Street Outreach/Mobile Crises Response – Community Services Department (LaShonda White/Sam Vaughn 510-620-5404).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $50,000 housing program contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Makanai, LLC. If approved, this on-call contract would provide budget and reporting support for past housing programs. The contract is worth up to $50,000 and would last for three years, with an option to extend for one more year. This is not yet approved — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision — this contract affects city housing program administration, not direct business operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.b
Contract with Makanai, LLC for Budget and Reporting Support for Housing Programs
•  APPROVE an on-call contract with Makanai, LLC to provide budget and reporting support related to past housing programs in an amount not to exceed $50,000, for a three-year term with a term expiration of June 30, 2029, with one one-year extension option – Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Jesus Morales 510-620-6706).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $300K hearing officer contracts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposal to award on-call contracts to two law firms — Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law — to provide Administrative Hearing Officer services. An Administrative Hearing Officer is an independent person who hears disputes or appeals about local government decisions, like property or licensing issues. If approved, each firm could be paid up to $300,000 over a three-year term. The contracts are not yet in effect; council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you right now — watch for the council decision, as it could affect how property or licensing disputes are handled in Richmond.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.a
On-Call Contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law for Administrative Hearing Officer Services
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing on-call contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law, to provide Administrative Hearing Officer services, for an amount not to exceed $300,000 per firm, for a three-year term - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1283).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Fee waiver for WCCUSD Latino/x Graduation at Richmond Memorial Auditorium
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to waive all auditorium fees, staffing costs, and other associated costs for the West Contra Costa Unified School District's Latino/x Graduation ceremony at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026. If approved, the city manager would be authorized to use city resources—including overtime for police, fire, public works, and other departments—to support the event. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been voted on.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a
Waiver of Auditorium Fees, Staffing Costs, and Other Associated Costs to Support the West Contra Costa Unified School District Latino/x Graduation at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium and Convention Center
•  REQUEST the city manager issue fee waivers to West Contra Costa Unified School District for the use of the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026 for the Latino/x Graduation Ceremony or Ceremonies, and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to expend City resources to support the graduation, which may include overtime from Police, Fire, Public Works, Community Services, and other departments as required  - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $110,000 legal research contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services. The contract, worth up to $110,000, would cover the period from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029. It would provide the City Attorney's Office with access to Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal research tools. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.c
Contract with Thomson Reuters Enterprise for Legal Research Subscription Services
•  APPROVE a contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services for Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal in an amount not to exceed $110,000 for the term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, subject to non-substantive revisions approved by the City Attorney’s Office; and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to execute the contract – City Attorney’s Office (Christopher Dykzeul 510-620-6507/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $150,000 investigation contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations. If approved, the contract would allow the company to conduct confidential personnel investigations for the city from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029. The total cost would not exceed $150,000. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners — this is an internal city staffing matter.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.b
Legal Services Agreement with Ellis Investigations for Confidential Personnel Investigations
•  APPROVE a legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for a term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, for a total amount not to exceed $150,000 – City Attorney’s Office/Human Resources Department (Shannon Moore/Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $75K legal fee increase for Winehaven case
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed fourth amendment to a legal services agreement with the law firm Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC. If approved, this amendment would increase the total amount paid to the firm by $75,000 — bringing the total contract to no more than $450,000 — for legal work related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation and other connected matters. The proposed change would also extend the agreement through December 31, 2027. The $75,000 would come from the city's General Fund (the main pool of money used for everyday city services). This is not yet approved; council will consider and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action needed — watch for the council decision, as this could affect city legal spending priorities.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.a
Legal Services Agreement No. 4 with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC
•  APPROVE a fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC, for legal representation related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation and related matters, to increase compensation by $75,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $450,000, extend the term through December 31, 2027, and APPROPRIATE $75,000 from the General Fund to cover associated legal fees. – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).
